title: Psalms, Chapter 29
description: Psalms chapter 29. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 94. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 94.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>ψαλμοϲ τωδηϲ του εγʼκαινιϲμου του οικου τω δαυειδʼ <sup>2</sup>υψωϲω ϲε κε οτι ϋπελαβεϲ με και ουκ ευφραναϲ τουϲ εχθρουϲ μου επ εμοι <sup>3</sup>κε ο θϲ μου εκεκραξα προϲ ϲε και ϊαϲω με <sup>4</sup>κε ανηγαγεϲ εξ αδου την ψυχην μου εϲωϲαϲ με εκ των καταβαινοντων ειϲ λακκον <sup>5</sup>ψαλατε τω κω οι οϲιοι αυτου και εξομολογειϲθε τη μνημη τηϲ αγιωϲυνηϲ αυτου
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>Give unto the LORD, O ye mighty, give unto the LORD glory and strength. <sup>2</sup>Give unto the LORD the glory due unto his name; worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness. <sup>3</sup>The voice of the LORD
is upon the waters: the God of glory thundereth: the LORD
is upon many waters. <sup>4</sup>The voice of the LORD
is powerful; the voice of the LORD
is full of majesty. <sup>5</sup>The voice of the LORD breaketh the cedars; yea, the LORD breaketh the cedars of Lebanon.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>οτι οργη εν τω θυμω αυτου και ζωη ε τω θεληματι αυτου το εϲπεραϲ αυλιϲθηϲεται κλαυθμοϲ και ειϲ το πρωϊ αγαλλιαϲιϲ <sup>7</sup>εγω δε ειπα εν τη ευθηνια μου ου μη ϲαλευθω ειϲ τον αιωνα <sup>8</sup>κε εν τω θεληματι ϲου παραϲχου τω καλλι μου δυναμιν απεϲτρεψαϲ δε το προϲωπον ϲου και εγενηθην τεταραγμενοϲ <sup>9</sup>προϲ ϲε κε κεκραξομαι και προϲ τον θν μου δεηθηϲομαι <sup>10</sup>τιϲ ωφελεια εν τω αιματι μου εν τω καταβηναι με ειϲ διαφθοραν μη εξομολογηϲεται ϲοι χουϲ η αναγγελει την αληθειαν ϲου
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>He maketh them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young unicorn. <sup>7</sup>The voice of the LORD divideth the flames of fire. <sup>8</sup>The voice of the LORD shaketh the wilderness; the LORD shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh. <sup>9</sup>The voice of the LORD maketh the hinds to calve, and discovereth the forests: and in his temple doth every one speak of
his glory. <sup>10</sup>The LORD sitteth upon the flood; yea, the LORD sitteth King for ever.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>ηκουϲεν κϲ και ηλεηϲεν με κϲ εγενηθη βοηθοϲ μου <sup>12</sup>εϲτρεψαϲ τον κοπετον μου ειϲ χαραν εμοι διερρηξαϲ τον ϲακκον μου και περιεζωϲαϲ με ευφροϲυνην <sup>13</sup>οπωϲ αν ψαλη ϲοι η δοξα μου και ου μη κατανυγω κε ο θϲ μου ειϲ τον αιωνα εξομολογηϲομαι ϲοι
translation.text: <sup>11</sup>The LORD will give strength unto his people; the LORD will bless his people with peace.
